JOHNSON COUNTY CARE CENTER

Overview

Address
122 EAST MARKET STREET, WARRENSBURG, MO 64093
Phone
6607478101
Certified beds
87
Avg daily residents
74 (85% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1979-03-12
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 24.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 1.97 3.34 2.28 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.10 0.58 0.12 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.44 0.74 0.51 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.43 2.02 1.65 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 0.54 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
1.61
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.06
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
1.85
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
61.8%
annual
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.18
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.86
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-08-22 10 9 1 72 1 72
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-11-17 26 22 4 172 1 172
Total weighted health score
97.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (48)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
46 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2026-01-29 —
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. E 2024-08-22 2024-10-05
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-06-26 2023-06-13
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-06-26 2023-08-09
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-06-26 2023-08-09
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2023-03-14 2023-03-04
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0742 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. F 2022-11-17 2022-12-31
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. E 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2020-03-06 2020-04-19
